Defining Severe Diabetes Mellitus: A Consensus Framework for Grading and Staging Diabetes Based on Pathophysiology and Complications

evidence_entities.text: Diabetes mellitus is a group of metabolic disorders characterized by chronic hyperglycemia, with diverse etiologies and clinical courses
Low non-fasting C-peptide levels (<200 pmol/L) indicate severe insulin deficiency and are associated with greater glycemic variability and hypoglycemia risk
Stage 4 represents irreversible, disabling, or life-threatening complications, reflecting extensive systemic involvement
We define severe diabetes mellitus, according to the DGSC, as a metabolic grade of 3 or higher or a complication stage of 3 or higher
